_note: Hand-verified ground truth (checked against each app's real developer docs). truth.access_model is Self-Serve if a developer can self-issue free/trial credentials, else Gated. Used by `python research.py --accuracy-movement` to score first-pass vs post-verification.

truth.notes: developer.copper.com — REST API; API key self-issued from account settings (trial available).
plain.com/docs — GraphQL API; API key from workspace settings.
Pumble REST API with API token.
Free developer org; REST + OAuth2.
Marketing API behind Meta app review + business verification.
Good REST API but keys only issuable by an admin on an existing paid site.
REST API; access tied to being a Gladly customer.
Cloud API exists but requires Meta business verification + app review for production.
Requires a developer token approval + OAuth.
Marketing Developer Platform / partner approval required.
Standard API access requires app review; trial access is limited.
Instant test keys; REST.
REST + GraphQL; self-serve tokens.
REST; internal integration token or OAuth.
REST; self-serve app + OAuth.
REST; self-serve account SID + auth token.
Paid Professional Seller + developer approval.
developers.hubspot.com authentication overview: OAuth for public apps and static access tokens for single-account apps.
shopify.dev API authentication: OAuth/session-token exchange and app access tokens; partner and development-store flow is self-serve.
Zendesk developer docs: verified users use email plus API token or OAuth access token.
Intercom authentication docs: private-app access tokens and OAuth for public multi-workspace apps.
Mailchimp Marketing API quick start: create an account, generate an API key, and use OAuth 2 for other users.
Discord developer docs: self-serve application registration, bot token, and OAuth2 flows.
Cloudflare API docs: users create API tokens in the dashboard and call the REST API with Authorization: Bearer API_TOKEN.
Vercel REST API docs: create access tokens in account settings and send them as Authorization: Bearer.
Airtable official docs: personal access tokens for account access and OAuth integrations for access on behalf of users.
Asana authentication docs list personal access tokens and OAuth for API integrations.
